svg.text: SOAR-Light On-Prem — Complete Data Flow Walkthrough
Example: NIOS Grid "nios-grid-01" → DNS query logs → On-Prem SOAR-Light Flow #23809 → Python script → Block malicious IPs
STAGE 1 — Flow Created in Cloud, Config Generated for On-Prem Delivery
☁️ CLOUD (Same as C2C: User creates flow via CSP Portal)
STAGE 2 — NIOS Grid Generates DNS Data → On-Prem Collection
Source: SpoolDir watching dns-in output directory
Example: Raw NIOS Syslog → Parsed CSV
Apr 1 14:23:05 nios-grid-01 named: query: evil.com IN A + (10.0.1.5)
Schema: {timestamp: STRING, query: STRING, qtype: STRING,
client_ip: STRING, source_host: STRING, log_type: STRING}
Watches parquet directory → detects new files → assigns to worker → worker reads parquet
→ streams binary data via gRPC to cloud data.exporter.kafka service
RegisterDataExporterKafkaServer() → Receives parquet binary → Deserializes rows → Converts each row to JSON
STAGE 4 — Cloud Kafka → On-Prem gRPC-IN (Bidirectional Stream Bridge)
Bidirectional gRPC stream: Subscribe(stream ServerCtrl) returns (stream ClientCtrl)
Reads JSON messages, wraps in protobuf Message{TopicIndex, Data, Identifier}
STAGE 5 — soar-light On-Prem Binary Receives Config & Spawns Python
⚠️ KEY DIFFERENCE vs C2C: On-prem uses --grpc_host=cdc-grpc-in:9195 instead of --kafka_consumer=kafka1:9092. The Python code detects this and uses gRPC consumer instead of Kafka consumer.
STAGE 6 — Python gRPC Consumer (Data Arrives at User's Script)
Note: On-prem topics use flowId_dataType (no accountId prefix)
3. for resp in client_ctrl: # blocking recv
ACK (Acknowledgment) Flow — After script processes each message:
Python script → MarkMessages(Identifier=offset, ErrorReason="") → gRPC stream → cdc.grpc-in server → dsclient → cloud data.exporter.kafka → Kafka offset commit
If script raises exception: MarkMessages(ErrorReason="Processing failed: ...") → message may be retried
If no data received → close stream → reconnect to gRPC-in
Prevents cloud proxy from closing idle connection
STAGE 7 — External Destination (User's Script Takes Automated Action)
COMPLETE ON-PREM DATA JOURNEY — End-to-End Summary
User creates SOAR-Light flow in cloud CSP Portal (#23809)
config.generator → config.manager → writes /etc/config/soar_light.json on OPH appliance
NIOS Grid generates DNS query syslog files
Compressed .gz files → SCP to cdc.dns-in → Parse syslog → CSV records
Apache Flume batches CSV → Parquet files
SpoolDir source → memory channel → Parquet sink → /cloud-out/dns/*.parquet
cdc.grpc-out uploads Parquet to cloud data.exporter.kafka
Worker pool → gRPC binary stream → Cloud receives → Deserialize → Produce to Kafka topic "acme-corp_flowID23809_DDI_QUERY_RESP_LOG"
cdc.grpc-in bridges cloud Kafka → on-prem via bidirectional gRPC
dsclient subscribes to cloud DataStream → receives Messages → stores in StreamCache → serves to Python via local gRPC server (:9195)
soar-light on-prem binary watches config file → spawns Python per data type
inotify → parse soar_light.json → CreateVirtualEnv → Generate driver → Exec: python3 --grpc_host=cdc-grpc-in:9195
Python subscribes to gRPC-in, receives JSON events, executes user's script
User's script takes automated action (block IP, create ticket, update RPZ)
context has auth tokens + variables → requests.post() / firewall API / NIOS WAPI call
NIOS Syslog (.gz) → cdc.dns-in (CSV) → Flume (Parquet) → grpc-out (gRPC binary) → data.exporter.kafka (JSON→Kafka)
→ grpc-in dsclient (protobuf Message) → gRPC server (ClientCtrl) → Python (json.loads → dict) → User script → HTTP POST to destination
Key Identifiers for This Example Flow:
CDC SOAR-Light On-Prem Flow — Detailed Walkthrough with Example Flow #23809 from NIOS Grid
